confirmed_issues.description: OHLC validation is missing on candle ingestion. The PHP handler stores candles with any non-zero OHLCV fields without verifying that high >= max(open,close) and low <= min(open,close). A logically invalid candle (e.g. low > close, or high < open) could be stored and used in signal engine calculations.
confirmed_issues.root_cause: insert_mt5_candle() and the candle pre-validation block in post_ea_market_stream() only check that the four OHLC keys exist, not that their relative ordering is valid.
confirmed_issues.proposed_fix: Add OHLC consistency guard in the candle pre-validation block: reject if high < max(open,close) or low > min(open,close), with structured 400 error and audit trail.

confirmed_issues.description: npm run lint fails with 90 prettier formatting errors across src/ routes. All errors are whitespace/formatting only (prettier/prettier rule), not logic errors. The build succeeds. This is a pre-existing cosmetic issue.
confirmed_issues.root_cause: Prettier formatting not enforced on some route files (src/routes/admin.tsx, src/routes/index.tsx, src/routes/plan.tsx, etc.).
confirmed_issues.proposed_fix: Run npx prettier --write src/ to auto-fix all formatting. No logic changes required.

confirmed_issues.description: The workflow specification describes a payload contract using fields quote_time, server_time, and candles[] (array). The actual EA (MarketDataEngine.mqh) sends timestamp, candle (singular object), and candle_m15 (singular object). The PHP handler is aligned with what the EA actually sends. No code bug — documentation divergence only.
confirmed_issues.root_cause: Spec was written describing a proposed API shape; the actual implementation evolved to use singular candle objects.
confirmed_issues.proposed_fix: Update parity audit documentation to reflect actual payload contract.

confirmed_correct: permission_ea_market_stream: X-EA-API-Key validated via hash_equals, all 4 header aliases handled, 401/503/403/400/403 error codes correct
wp_set_current_user() called before returning true from permission callback
stale data rejection at >300s returns error code stale_data with HTTP 400
candle staleness enforced separately at 180s in insert_mt5_candle()
signal engine gates on price_source=mt5 AND price_state=live AND price_age<=staleThresholdSec
backendConfirmed only set when status=READY && data_live
post_execute_signals rejects signals where backend_confirmed!=1 or status!=READY
FreshnessBadge uses state from backend, not self-computed age
get_cached_price() computes age_sec from stored updated_at (broker timestamp), not fetch time
authority-diagnostics requires WP session login (401 for unauthenticated) — correct and intentional
all 6 PHP regression test suites pass
